title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
problem_statement: Find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in an integer array.
intuition: Use patience sorting with binary search to maintain smallest endings for increasing subsequences.

approaches.name: DP with binary search
approaches.description: Maintain a tails array where tails[len] is the smallest ending value of an increasing subsequence of length len+1.
approaches.why_not: Simple O(n^2) DP is easier but too slow for large inputs.

interview_qa.q: What is the tails array in LIS?
interview_qa.a: It stores the smallest possible tail value for increasing subsequences of each length.
To find where the current number can extend or replace an existing subsequence in O(log n) time.
